topics.title: Emergency Fund Basics
topics.content: An emergency fund is your financial safety net. Aim for 3-6 months of expenses. Keep it in liquid, low-risk options like savings accounts or liquid funds.

topics.title: Power of Compounding
topics.description: How small investments grow into wealth over time
topics.content: Compound interest is 'interest on interest'. Start early - even ₹1,000/month at 12% returns becomes ₹1.2 lakhs in 10 years, ₹7.8 lakhs in 20 years!

topics.title: Fixed Deposits (FD)
topics.content: FDs offer guaranteed returns (5-7% typically). Safe for short-term goals. Consider tax-saving FDs for additional benefits. Compare rates across banks.

getting_started.name: Getting Started with Investing
getting_started.description: Your first steps into the investment world

topics.title: Understanding SIP
topics.content: SIP is like a gym membership for your money. Invest fixed amounts monthly, regardless of market conditions. This 'rupee-cost averaging' reduces risk and removes timing decisions.

topics.title: Mutual Funds 101
topics.content: Mutual funds pool money from many investors to invest in stocks/bonds. Fund managers make investment decisions. Types: Equity (stocks), Debt (bonds), Hybrid (both).

topics.title: Risk vs Return
topics.description: Understanding the relationship between risk and rewards
topics.content: Higher returns come with higher risk. Conservative investors (low risk) get 5-7% returns. Aggressive investors (high risk) can get 12-15% long-term returns. Know your risk appetite!

topics.title: Diversification Strategy
topics.description: Don't put all eggs in one basket
topics.content: Diversification spreads risk across different assets. Mix equity funds, debt funds, gold. Different sectors, market caps, geographies. Reduces volatility while maintaining returns.

topics.title: Goal-Based Investing
topics.description: Invest with specific financial goals in mind
topics.content: Different goals need different strategies. Short-term (1-3 years): FDs/Liquid funds. Medium-term (3-7 years): Balanced funds. Long-term (7+ years): Equity savings.

topics.title: Market Cycles & Patience
topics.description: Markets go up and down - stay calm
topics.content: Markets have cycles: Bull (up) and Bear (down) markets. Historical data shows markets recover. Long-term investors benefit from staying invested through volatility.

topics.title: ELSS Funds
topics.description: Tax-saving mutual funds with equity exposure
topics.content: ELSS funds offer tax deduction under 80C (up to ₹1.5 lakh). 3-year lock-in period. Equity-oriented, potential for higher returns than traditional tax-saving options.

topics.title: Index Funds & ETFs
topics.content: Index funds/ETFs track market indices like Nifty 50. Low expense ratios (0.1-0.5%). No fund manager bias. Good for long-term, buy-and-hold investors.

topics.title: Cost of Investing
topics.content: Key costs: Expense ratio (0.5-2%), Exit load (if redeemed early), Transaction charges. Higher costs reduce your returns. Compare before investing.

aggressive_beginner.name: High Growth Path
aggressive_beginner.description: For those willing to take calculated risks

milestones.name: Strategy Ready
milestones.reward: Can create a basic investment plan

quick_tips: Start with emergency fund before investing
Invest only what you can afford to lose
